sutta: once the exalted 1 dwelt near badiya in jatia groot and there ukaha bandarka's grandson paid him a visit and after saluting sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord let the exalted 1 accept a meal at my house tomorrow he as the fourth with us tree the exalted 1 accepted by his silence then ogaha mendaka's grandson seeing the exalted 1 had accepted rose from his seat saluted and took his leave keeping the exalted 1 on his right now when the night was over the exalted 1 robin himself in the morning took bowl and cloaked and went to uggaha's house and there sat down on the seat made ready and uh dhaka's grandson served and satisfied the exalted 1 by hand with plenty of heart and soft food and when the exalted 1 had removed his hand from his bowl he sat down at 1 side thus seated he said lord these girls of mine will be going to the husband's families lord let the exalted 1 counsel them let the exalted 1 advise them for their good and happiness for many a day then the exalted 1 spoke to them and said wherefore girls train yourselves in this way to whatsoever husband our parents give us wishing our will seeking our happiness compassionate because of compassion for him we will rise up early be the last to retire be willing workers order all things sweetly and be gentle voiced train yourselves thus girls and in in this way also girls we will honor revere esteem and respect all whom our husband reveres whether mother or father recruits our holy man and on their arrival will offer them a seed and water train yourselves with us girls and in this way also girls we will be depthed and nimble at our husbands home crops whether they be of wool or cotton making it our business to understand the world so as to do and get it done train yourselves thus girls and in this way also girls whatsoever our husband's household consists of slaves messengers and workforce we will know the work of each by what has been done their remissness by what has not been done we will know the strength and the weakness of the sick we will divide the hard and soft food each according to his share train yourselves thus girls and in this way also girls the money corn silver and gold that our husband brings home we will keep safe watch and ward over it and act as no rubber thief spendthrift wastrel therein train yourselves thus girls indeed girls possess of these 5 qualities women on the breaking up of the body after death are reborn among the devas of lovely form that's the end of the suta
commentary: this is 1 of the sutas which is completely devoted to laypersons it is not a deep suta is a very practical suta where the buddha is advising this young girls what is about to be married or just married that they should do their duty as a housewife first 1 is to be good to their husbands and rise up early sleep be the last to to sleep the gentle voice etc and the second 1 whoever the husband respects her whether it's mother or father or a man etc then the wife also should do the same and the third is that she should be skilled in the husband's business now because in those days and their business was some home craft of wool or cotton etc and then she should train herself also and the fourth is that whatever the household workers that she she should know to judge their work and to give them their food according to their share then the fifth 1 is not to waste whatever money the husband brings home to spend it carefully and look after it so um in the former days it was like this where women were in a way sort of a place second fiddle to the men and in those days because women were a bit more submissive then they tended to be more um how do you say you know not so much divorce not so much fighting and quarrels in the home and because the man wears the pants in the house and then women have to be submissive but nowadays a lot of women work so sometimes it causes some problems because sometimes women tend to wear the pants in the house and but whether it's the whole system of the new system everything has this good and bad points now now the next is
